channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Panama Canal administrator pushes back against Trump's assertions of Chinese meddling
item.description: PANAMA CITY (AP) — The administrator of the Panama Canal said Friday that the vital waterway will remain in Panamanian hands and open to commerce from all countries, rejecting claims by President-elect Donald Trump that the United States should take it over.
item.dc:creator: ALMA SOLÍS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Supreme Court to weigh reinstating Obamacare care requirements struck down by lower court
item.description: The Supreme Court agreed Friday to consider some reinstating preventative care coverage requirements under the Affordable Care Act that were struck down by a lower court.
item.dc:creator: Lindsay Whitehurst, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: FBI must be independent and above the partisan fray, outgoing director says in farewell address
item.description: Outgoing FBI Director Christopher Wray says the bureau must remain independent, above the partisan fray and “committed to upholding the rule of law.
item.dc:creator: Eric Tucker, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Biden claim about empty hydrants undermined by LA water utility's lack of common wildfire safety protocol
item.description: President Biden on Thursday said that empty fire hydrants in the Los Angeles area were the result of an intentional power shutoff, but a report indicates the city's water utility might lack a common safety protocol.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Biden to deliver prime-time farewell to nation on Wednesday from Oval Office
item.description: President Joe Biden will deliver a farewell address to the nation Wednesday from the Oval Office, five days before President-elect Donald Trump is sworn in.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Tulsi Gabbard changes tune on controversial intelligence tool following GOP lobbying
item.description: Tulsi Gabbard went back on her previous position against section 702 of the Foreign Intelligence Surveillance Act ahead of her confirmation hearing in the U.S. Senate.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: DHS extends TPS for Salvadorans, potentially complicating Trump's plans
item.description: The Biden administration on Friday extended a key humanitarian program for El Salvadoran nationals in a move that could complicate the incoming Trump administration’s plans to do away with long-term immigrant protections.
item.dc:creator: Rafael Bernal, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Investigation clears Duluth mayor’s campaign manager and partner
item.description: An external investigation commissioned by the city of Duluth has found Mayor Roger Reinert’s partner and campaign manager Amber Gurske did not act improperly through her communications with city staff early in his administration.
item.dc:creator: Dan Kraker, MPR News

item.title: Trump is sentenced in hush money case
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump received an unconditional discharge for his criminal conviction, meaning he will get a criminal record but no other penalties.
item.dc:creator: Ximena Bustillo, MPR News

item.title: Judge holds Rudy Giuliani in contempt of court for continued lies about Georgia election workers
item.description: Rudy Giuliani has been found in contempt of court for continuing to spread lies about two former Georgia election workers after a jury awarded the women a $148 million defamation judgment.
item.dc:creator: Michael Kunzelman, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Sen. J.D. Vance resigns ahead of vice presidential inauguration
item.description: COLUMBUS, Ohio (WCMH) -- U.S. Sen. J.D. Vance resigned from his seat in Congress' upper chamber on Thursday ahead of his inauguration as vice president to President-elect Donald Trump.
item.dc:creator: Natalie Fahmy, NewsNation

item.title: Pennsylvania GOP Rep. Meuser considering run for governor
item.description: Rep. Dan Meuser (R-Pa.) signaled he is seriously considering challenging Pennsylvania Gov. Josh Shapiro (D) for his post in 2026. “I’m considering it,” Meuser told The Hill on Thursday. “I mean if I get in it’s because I think I can win and that means beating the incumbent which is Josh Shapiro.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Michelle Obama 'sends her thoughts and prayers' while absent from Carter's funeral
item.description: Michelle Obama wasn't among the mourners attending former President Carter's funeral service in Washington, but she says she's thinking of and praying for "everyone who loved and learned" from the late commander in chief.
item.dc:creator: Judy Kurtz, Fox 5

item.title: Lack of federal funds may mean no EBT replacements for scam victims
item.description: Many parents have reported benefits being erased from EBT cards in the weeks since Congress declined to continue funding.
item.dc:creator: Sean C. Davis, NewsNation

item.title: Mayor Eric Adams delivers 4th State of the City address
item.description: HARLEM, Manhattan (PIX11) – New York City Mayor Eric Adams delivered his fourth State of the City address at The Apollo in Harlem on Thursday. Adams discussed topics like public safety, the cost of living, and new initiatives to build more in the city.
item.dc:creator: Erin Pflaumer, PIX 11

item.title: Nicole Mitchell’s attorneys push back on effort to bar ‘senator’ mentions at burglary trial
item.description: Ahead of a Minnesota lawmaker’s trial on burglary charges, attorneys are going back and forth over whether she can be called “senator” in front of jurors.
item.dc:creator: MPR News Staff, MPR News

item.title: Biden says he could've beaten Trump in November
item.description: “It’s presumptuous to say that, but I think yes,” the president said when reporter Susan Page asked if he could have won.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, NewsNation

item.title: First Jan. 6 felony defendant allowed to attend Trump inauguration
item.description: U.S. District Judge Rudolph Contreras granted William Pope's motion despite objections from the Department of Justice.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, NewsNation

item.title: 2 death row inmates reject Biden's commutation of sentences
item.description: Shannon Agofsky and Len Davis believe the life sentences put them at a legal disadvantage with them planning to seek appeals based on claims of innocence.
item.dc:creator: Kyler Swaim, NewsNation

item.title: Judge temporarily blocks release of special counsel report on Trump cases as court fight simmers
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A federal judge on Tuesday temporarily blocked the public release of special counsel Jack Smith's report on investigations into Donald Trump as an appeals court weighs a challenge to the disclosure of a much-anticipated document just days before the president-elect reclaims office.
item.dc:creator: ERIC TUCKER, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Board of Supervisors splits votes on new chair following Vargas' exit
item.description: The San Diego County Board of Supervisors punted a vote on who will succeed Nora Vargas as chair after competing motions to elevate either Terra Lawson-Remer or Jim Desmond to the role failed in votes down partisan lines.
item.dc:creator: Danielle Dawson, Fox 5

item.title: Trump refuses to rule out use of military force to take control of Greenland, Panama Canal
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump on Tuesday said he would not rule out the use of military force to seize control of the Panama Canal and Greenland, as he declared U.S. control of both to be vital to American national security.
item.dc:creator: WILL WEISSERT and ZEKE MILLER Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: California governor proposes $322B budget with no deficit after two years of shortfalls
item.description: California Gov. Gavin Newsom has proposed a $322 billion budget without a deficit, a welcome change after two years of significant budget shortfalls in the nation's most populous state.
item.dc:creator: Tran Nguyen, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Biden creates 2 new national monuments, setting a conservation record
item.description: The White House says President Joe Biden has now protected a total of 674 million acres of lands and waters — a record for any administration. This includes two new national monuments in California.
item.dc:creator: Deepa Shivaram, MPR News

item.title: U.K. leader Starmer slams ‘lies and misinformation’ after attacks from Elon Musk
item.description: British Prime Minister Keir Starmer on Monday condemned "lies and misinformation" that he said are undermining U.K. democracy, in response to a barrage of attacks on his government from Elon Musk.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Rudy Giuliani is held in contempt of court in $148 million defamation case
item.description: Rudy Giuliani was found in contempt of court for failing to properly respond to requests for information as he turned over assets to satisfy a defamation ruling granted to two Georgia poll workers.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Judge holds Giuliani in contempt in Georgia election workers case
item.description: A federal judge held Rudy Giuliani in civil contempt on Monday for failing to comply with court orders in two former Georgia election workers’ efforts to collect their $146 million defamation judgment. At the end of a two-day hearing that saw Giuliani take the stand, U.S.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, Fox 5

item.title: Millions of public workers are set to get higher Social Security benefits. Here's why
item.description: Public service workers, including some teachers, firefighters and police officers, may soon see their Social Security payments increase by hundreds of dollars monthly.
item.dc:creator: Juliana Kim, MPR News

item.title: Congress certifies Trump's election win despite weather threat
item.description: Congress certified President-elect Donald Trump’s election win on Monday as Speaker Mike Johnson (R-La.) plowed ahead with the vote despite a snowstorm set to hit Washington, D.C.
item.dc:creator: Mychael Schnell, Fox 5

item.title: Wisconsin’s Evers revives plan to enable voters to repeal and create state laws
item.description: Wisconsin Gov. Tony Evers is reviving his plan to enable voters to repeal and create state laws, this time through the state budget.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Trump is certified as the 2024 election winner without challenge, in stark contrast to 2021 violence
item.description: Lawmakers convened under heavy security and a snowstorm to certify the vote, with the reading of the electoral results from the states coming swiftly.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Judge denies Jan. 6 rioter’s bid to attend Trump inauguration
item.description: A U.S. District Judge denied a Jan. 6 rioter’s request to attend President-elect Trump's inauguration later this month, citing concerns over the Capitol insurrection which incited hours of chaos and violence in the nation’s capital.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, WFLA, NBC
